ddls:
CREATE TABLE "traffic_data" (
  "time_id" INTEGER /* Timestamp identifier for the data */,
  "crosswalk_L" INTEGER /* Number of pedestrians at the left crosswalk */,
  "crosswalk_R" INTEGER /* Number of pedestrians at the right crosswalk */,
  "crosswalk_U" INTEGER /* Number of pedestrians at the upper crosswalk */,
  "crosswalk_B" INTEGER /* Number of pedestrians at the bottom crosswalk */,
  "crosswalk_T" INTEGER /* Number of pedestrians at the top crosswalk */,
  "cars_L" INTEGER /* Number of cars at the left lane */,
  "cars_R" INTEGER /* Number of cars at the right lane */,
  "cars_U" INTEGER /* Number of cars at the upper lane */,
  "cars_B" INTEGER /* Number of cars at the bottom lane */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* Weather condition at the time of data collection */,
  "visibility" TEXT /* Visibility condition at the time of data collection */,
  "traffic_light_status" TEXT /* Status of traffic lights */,
  "speed_limit" INTEGER /* Speed limit at the intersection */,
  "average_speed" INTEGER /* Average speed of vehicles */,
  "vehicle_types" TEXT /* Types of vehicles detected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("time_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "intersection_name" TEXT /* Name of the intersection where the sensor is installed */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was installed */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor */,
  "sensor_status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "battery_level" INTEGER /* Battery level of the sensor (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "intersections" (
  "intersection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each intersection */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the intersection */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the intersection */,
  "number_of_lanes" INTEGER /* Number of lanes at the intersection */,
  "crosswalk_count" INTEGER /* Number of crosswalks at the intersection */,
  "pedestrian_signal" TEXT /* Type of pedestrian signal */,
  "traffic_light_count" INTEGER /* Number of traffic lights at the intersection */,
  "speed_bump_count" INTEGER /* Number of speed bumps at the intersection */,
  "last_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the last inspection */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Maintenance schedule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("intersection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_logs" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that triggered the alert */,
  "time_id" INTEGER /* Timestamp identifier for when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., accident, congestion) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert situation */,
  "response_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to respond to the alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert */,
  "alert_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the alert in minutes */,
  "alert_resolution" TEXT /* Description of how the alert was resolved */,
  "incident_location" TEXT /* Specific location of the incident */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_time_id FOREIGN KEY ("time_id") REFERENCES traffic_data ("time_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "emergency_services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each emergency service */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency service */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the emergency service */,
  "response_time" INTEGER /* Average response time in minutes */,
  "service_type" TEXT /* Type of emergency service */,
  "response_radius" INTEGER /* Radius in which the service can respond quickly */,
  "last_response_time" INTEGER /* Last recorded response time in minutes */,
  "service_area" TEXT /* Area where the service operates */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)
